title: Layer 0 + Tier 1: The Trinitarian Core — Proof Explorer
executive_summary: A1.1 Existence Something exists A1.3 Other Exists Relation A2.1 Substrate Req. Information needs a carrier A2.2 Self-Grounding Regress terminates A5.1 Observation Info needs observer BC4 Three Observers Trinity terminus BC6 Infinite Source Open system Everything else — all 188 axioms, all theorems, all predictions — derives from the claim that distinguishable things exist in something. The packet separates publishable language from unresolved math, axiom, and contradiction obligations.
plain_english: A1.1 Existence Something exists A1.3 Other Exists Relation A2.1 Substrate Req. Information needs a carrier A2.2 Self-Grounding Regress terminates A5.1 Observation Info needs observer BC4 Three Observers Trinity terminus BC6 Infinite Source Open system The entire Theophysics framework compresses to two Layer 0 primitives . Everything else — all 188 axioms, all theorems, all predictions — derives from the claim that distinguishable things exist in something. The seven Tier 1 axioms unpack what that logically requires. Not all claims share the same epistemic status. Conflating them weakens the case. Every axiom is tagged with its tier — how it is justified, not how confident we are in it. In simpler terms, the article is trying to establish: Everything else — all 188 axioms, all theorems, all predictions — derives from the claim that distinguishable things exist in something. The math layer has been extracted separately so equations can be translated and checked without blurring them into the prose.

math_items.expression: Experimental protocols with σ thresholds, replication, pre-registration.
axiom distinction_exists : ∃ (a b : S), a ≠ b

Without plurality, there is no information, no measurement, no physics. Shannon entropy H(X) = −Σ p(x) log p(x) requires distinguishable outcomes. This is the relational axiom: reality is not monistic.
axiom other_exists : ∃ (a b : S), a ≠ b
effective : ∀ (o : O) (a b : S), a ≠ b → registers o a b
"N_observers = 3 is the minimal closure model for Subject-Object-Relation adjudication."
∃ (o&sub1; o&sub2; o&sub3; : O), o&sub1; ≠ o&sub2; ∧ o&sub2; ≠ o&sub3; ∧ o&sub1; ≠ o&sub3;
let redemption := if s.disordered ≥ k then k else s.disordered
(∃ (a b : S), a ≠ b) → ∃ (a b : S), a ≠ b := id

Distinction is the minimal bridge between "something exists" and "something can be identified." Denying it is self-refuting: claiming "distinctions don't exist" requires distinguishing that claim from its negation. -- Lean4 formalization axiom distinction_exists : ∃ (a b : S), a ≠ b Defeat Conditions Self-refuting to deny — any denial requires distinguishing the claim from its negation QM is fundamentally a theory...

No counterexample has ever been found. -- Lean4 formalization axiom substrate_exists : Nonempty S Defeat Conditions Show information existing without any substrate Show causal efficacy of pure abstraction Falsify Landauer's principle (experimentally confirmed: Berut 2012, Jun 2014, Gavrilov 2016) Tier 1 — The Seven Irreducible Axioms A1.1 — Chain Position 1/188 Existence "Something exists rather than nothing." Quantum vacuum |0⟩ is not "nothing" — it has structure, energy density ~10 −9 J/m³, virtual fluctuations. -- Lean4: Existence = Nonempty substrate axiom existence : Nonempty S Defeat Conditions Self-refuting to deny — any denial requires an arguer, argument, and claim (all existing things) Modal logic: existence is necessary — no possible world with nothing ZFC: even the empty set itself exists Trinitarian Mapping...

This is the relational axiom: reality is not monistic. -- Lean4: At least two distinct elements axiom other_exists : ∃ (a b : S), a ≠ b Trinitarian Mapping Son — Distinction / Logos / Word A2.1 — Chain Position 8/188 Substrate Requirement "Information requires a substrate for instantiation." Since matter itself derives from information (Wheeler's "It from Bit"), matter cannot be the ultimate substrate.
